feed.title: Machine Learning for Precision Neuropsychiatry
feed.subtitle: Research lab on applications of machine learning for precision neuropsychiatry.

entry.title: New Publication in Nature Communications Biology, Normative Modeling of Brain Dynamics

link.title: New Publication in Nature Communications Biology, Normative Modeling of Brain Dynamics

entry.content:
We are excited to share our latest publication in Communications Biology (Nature Portfolio):

Normative modeling of brain dynamics across the lifespan

Understanding variability in brain function is a central challenge in neuroscience and psychiatry. In this study, we introduce a large-scale normative modeling framework to characterize brain dynamics across the human lifespan using MEG data.

By leveraging multi-site datasets and advanced machine learning techniques, the study establishes normative ranges of oscillatory brain activity across different frequency bands. These models capture how brain dynamics evolve with age and provide a reference against which individual deviations can be quantified.

The results demonstrate that:

  • Brain oscillations follow systematic, age-related trajectories across the lifespan.
  • Normative modeling can identify individual deviations from typical brain function.
  • These deviations hold promise as biomarkers for neuropsychiatric conditions.

This work represents an important step toward precision neuropsychiatry, where individualized assessments of brain function can support diagnosis, prognosis, and treatment selection.

This publication is closely connected to ongoing efforts in the lab, including the MEGaNorm initiative, aimed at charting normative brain function at multiple temporal, spatial, and individual scales.

We invite you to read the full article here:
https://www.nature.com/articles/s42003-026-09825-2
entry.summary: We are excited to share our latest publication in Communications Biology (Nature Portfolio):

entry.title: Welcoming Melissa Zandstra as a Postdoctoral Researcher

link.title: Welcoming Melissa Zandstra as a Postdoctoral Researcher

entry.content:
We are pleased to welcome Melissa Zandstra to the ML4PNP lab as a postdoctoral researcher.

Melissa will lead the project “Normative Range of P600 Morphology as a Diagnostic and Prognostic Tool in Neuropsychiatric Disorders.” This project is funded within the NWO NWA – Research along Routes programme. The project aims to establish normative ranges for the P600, an event-related potential associated with language processing, using large-scale EEG data and advanced computational methods.More information about the project can be found on the NWO project page.

We are excited to have Melissa join the lab and look forward to the contributions her work will bring to the ML4PNP research program.
entry.summary: We are pleased to welcome Melissa Zandstra to the ML4PNP lab as a postdoctoral researcher.

entry.title: ML4PNP Lab Website Is Now Online

link.title: ML4PNP Lab Website Is Now Online

entry.content:
We are pleased to announce the launch of the ML4PNP lab website. The site provides an overview of our research, projects, software, publications, and team, and will serve as a central place for sharing updates, outputs, and opportunities related to our work.

We will use this page to post news about new publications, project milestones, software releases, events, and other activities from the lab. More updates will follow soon.
entry.summary: We are pleased to announce the launch of the ML4PNP lab website. The site provides an overview of our research, projects, software, publications, and team, and will serve as a central place for sharing updates, outputs, and opportunities related to our work.
